user_message: Just came out of executive brief with Blue Yonder Airlines. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-03-23
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft), Kevin (Blue Yonder Airlines)

[00:01:15] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is executive brief.

[00:07:00] Kevin (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:12:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:23:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:26:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-N trap (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-N failure mode (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-N -- Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
